metadata.title: Write the Post-Build Document for a Classroom Tool with Claude Code

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Jambo, Liam — in for Bear. How does write the post-build document for a classroom tool with claude code? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.command: Write the Post-Build Document for a Classroom Tool with Claude Code?
props.segment: Write the Post-Build Document for a

beats.narration: What you built, what you delegated, what you kept, what you'd do differently. The five-section post-build document is the evidence your workflow is trustworthy.

The post-build document is not about proving AI use. It is about proving that the teacher's supervisory judgment was the deliverable. The sections record where the line was drawn, not just what was generated.
Ask Claude to generate a five-section post-build document for the sorting simulator.

props.command:
claude "Generate post-build document for sorting simulator.
  Five sections:
  (1) What was built (artifact + deployed path)
  (2) Surface-routing (Claude tasks vs human-only)
  (3) Delegation log (gate checks run, errors caught)
  (4) Verification evidence (checks passed, fixes)
  (5) Reflection (one concrete change next build)"

beats.narration: Five sections: artifact and deploy path, surface-routing naming human-only palette decisions, delegation log with gate check and CDN fix, verification evidence, and a concrete reflection for the next build.

props.code:
# Post-Build Document — Sorting Simulator

## 1. What Was Built
index.html — single-file sorting simulator
Deployed: rsync → school.edu/cs/sorting/

## 2. Surface-Routing
Claude: HTML/CSS/JS generation, color application
Human-only: DESIGN.md palette decisions,
  pedagogical annotation content, final QA

## 3. Delegation Log
Gate check: lint-html exit 0 ✓
Error caught: Google Fonts CDN blocked → inlined

## 4. Verification Evidence
Functional: all 5 checks passed
Environment: 1 failure caught and fixed pre-deploy

## 5. Reflection
Next build: write DESIGN.md contrast requirement
before generation, not after.

beats.narration: The five sections are complete. Surface-routing names one human-only task — palette decisions — and the reason it stayed human: aesthetic judgment about classroom context. Delegation log names a specific gate check. Reflection names a concrete process change, not a vague intention.
Score the delegation log on four dimensions to verify it is complete.

props.command:
claude "Score the delegation log on four dimensions:
  task routed, gate check run,
  error corrected, verification evidence.
  Does the log score all four?"

beats.narration: Scoring: task routed — HTML generation to Claude, yes. Gate check — lint-html exit 0, yes. Error corrected — Google Fonts CDN fix documented, yes. Verification evidence — functional and environment checks cited, yes. Four out of four. The log is complete and could serve as an AI-use disclosure to a supervisor.

The post-build document is the paper trail that makes the next build more defensible than the last. Write it while the decisions are fresh — thirty minutes after deployment, not three weeks later when you can't remember which gate check caught the CDN issue.
Next: design a classroom activity around the dangerous middle.
Like and subscribe for more Claude Code for Teachers.
